# Boyce

> Semantic protocol and safety layer for agentic database workflows.

Boyce gives AI agents structured database intelligence to generate correct, safe SQL — deterministically. It is an MCP server exposing 7 tools. The SQL compiler uses zero LLM calls — same inputs produce identical SQL, byte-for-byte, every time.

## Install

Run `pip install boyce` to install. Add live DB adapter: `pip install "boyce[postgres]"` (adds asyncpg for Postgres/Redshift).

## First Run

Run `boyce init` to auto-detect your MCP host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code, JetBrains, Windsurf) and write the mcpServers config. Or configure manually — see llms-full.txt.

## MCP Configuration (no API key needed for MCP hosts)

Add boyce to your mcpServers config: command is `boyce`, env var `BOYCE_DB_URL` is your PostgreSQL DSN (optional — omit for schema-only mode; EXPLAIN pre-flight returns `"unchecked"` without it).

MCP hosts (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) do not need a Boyce API key. The host LLM calls `get_schema` to read the schema, constructs a StructuredFilter, and calls `ask_boyce` (Mode A). Only `ask_boyce` Mode B/C requires `BOYCE_PROVIDER` + `BOYCE_MODEL`.

## MCP Tools

- `ingest_source`: Parse SemanticSnapshot from dbt, LookML, DDL, SQLite, Django, SQLAlchemy, Prisma, CSV, Parquet
- `ingest_definition`: Store certified business definitions (auto-injected at query time)
- `get_schema`: Return full schema context + StructuredFilter docs for host LLM reasoning
- `ask_boyce`: Tri-modal NL→SQL with NULL trap detection + EXPLAIN pre-flight (Mode A: StructuredFilter, Mode B: NL+credentials, Mode C: NL fallback)
- `validate_sql`: Validate hand-written SQL — EXPLAIN pre-flight, Redshift lint, NULL risk check — without executing
- `query_database`: Read-only SELECT execution
- `profile_data`: Column profiling (null %, distinct count, min/max)

## Supported Databases

Redshift, Postgres, DuckDB, BigQuery (dialect-aware SQL generation)

## Key Facts

- Python 3.10+ required
- MIT licensed
- Uses LiteLLM for NL→SQL (supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, 100+ providers)
- MCP hosts do NOT need an API key — host LLM calls get_schema + ask_boyce (Mode A) directly
- 10 source parsers with auto-detection via `boyce scan`
- Setup wizard: `boyce init` (detects Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code, JetBrains, Windsurf)

## Detailed Reference

- [Full agent docs](https://convergentmethods.com/boyce/llms-full.txt): Complete tool reference with parameters
- [The Null Trap](https://convergentmethods.com/boyce/null-trap/): Why NULL distributions silently corrupt agentic SQL — and why the fix isn't a better model
- [GitHub](https://github.com/boyce-io/boyce)
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/boyce/)
